question: What does perplexity measure in language modeling? If a model has perplexity of 50, what does it mean?
code_hint: Perplexity = avg number of choices the model is confused between, 50 means ~50 equally likely next tokens
expected_output: Perplexity 50 means the model has ~50 equally likely choices for next token on average
template:
# Perplexity interpretation:
# - Measures how well a probability model predicts a sample
# - Perplexity = exp(average log likelihood per token)
# - Lower perplexity = better model
# - Perplexity of 50 means ~50 equally likely choices on average

print("Perplexity 50 means the model has ~50 equally likely choices for next token on average")
answer:
# Perplexity interpretation:
# - Measures how well a probability model predicts a sample
# - Perplexity = exp(average log likelihood per token)
# - Lower perplexity = better model
# - Perplexity of 50 means ~50 equally likely choices on average

print("Perplexity 50 means the model has ~50 equally likely choices for next token on average")
